<<THE MAXIMUM LIKELIHOOD ESTIMATORS FOR THE PARAMETERS>>

THE MAXIMUM LIKELIHOOD ESTIMATORS FOR THE PARAMETERS ARE AS FOLLOWS:

THE MLE FOR MU_X IS THE SAMPLE MEAN OF THE PRE-TREATMENT BLOOD PRESSURES [X1, X2, ..., X50].

THE MLE FOR MU_Y IS THE SAMPLE MEAN OF THE POST-TREATMENT BLOOD PRESSURES [Y1, Y2, ..., Y50].

THE MLE FOR SIGMA_X2 IS THE SAMPLE VARIANCE OF THE PRE-TREATMENT BLOOD PRESSURES [X1, X2, ..., X50].

THE MLE FOR SIGMA_Y2 IS THE SAMPLE VARIANCE OF THE POST-TREATMENT BLOOD PRESSURES [Y1, Y2, ..., Y50].

THE MLE FOR SIGMA_XY IS THE COVARIANCE BETWEEN X_i AND Y_i [X1, X2, ..., X50] AND [Y1, Y2, ..., Y50].

THE TEST STATISTIC FOR THE HYPOTHESIS TEST IS THE F-RATIO [F-RATIO] CALCULATED USING THE DERIVED MLEs.

THE NULL HYPOTHESIS IS THAT THE DRUG HAS NO SIGNIFICANT EFFECT ON REDUCING BLOOD PRESSURE [NULL HYPOTHESIS].

THE ALTERNATIVE HYPOTHESIS IS THAT THE DRUG DOES HAVE A SIGNIFICANT EFFECT ON REDUCING BLOOD PRESSURE [ALTERNATIVE HYPOTHESIS].

THE REJECTION CRITERIA IS THAT IF THE F-RATIO IS GREATER THAN THE Critical F VALUE [REJECTION CRITERIA].

THE SIGNIFICANCE LEVEL IS 0.05 [SIGNIFICANCE LEVEL].

THE TEST STATISTIC IS THE F-RATIO [TEST STATISTIC].

THE REJECTION CRITERIA IS THAT IF THE F-RATIO IS GREATER THAN THE Critical F VALUE [REJECTION CRITERIA].

THE NULL HYPOTHESIS IS THAT THE DRUG HAS NO SIGNIFICANT EFFECT ON REDUCING BLOOD PRESSURE [NULL HYPOTHESIS].

THE ALTERNATIVE HYPOTHESIS IS THAT THE DRUG DOES HAVE A SIGNIFICANT EFFECT ON REDUCING BLOOD PRESSURE [ALTERNATIVE HYPOTHESIS].

THE REJECTION CRITERIA IS THAT IF THE F-RATIO IS GREATER THAN THE Critical F VALUE [REJECTION CRITERIA].
